mnemonics.title: Load Factor Threshold
mnemonics.content: Remember: '75 For Open Addressing' - Rehash when load factor exceeds 0.75 in open addressing.

mnemonics.title: Probing Formulas
mnemonics.content: LInear: h+i, Quad: h+i², Double: h+i×h2 - Think 'i² for quieter, h2 for double trouble'.

mnemonics.title: Chaining vs Open Addressing
mnemonics.content: CHAIN = CHaining uses Linked lists; OPEN = Option Places Elements iN table.

acronyms.usage: Used to analyze hash table performance: as α increases, operation time increases.
Linked list insertion in Separate Chaining often adds at head (LIFO) for O(1) insertion.

acronyms.expansion: Secondary hash function should never return zero
acronyms.usage: If h2(k)=0 in double hashing, step size becomes 0 causing infinite loop at initial position.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine cars (keys) trying to park in a linear row of parking spots. If their designated spot is taken, they move to the next spot, then next, until they find an empty spot. This creates clusters of cars.
Think of each hash table index as a bus stand (bucket). Multiple buses (keys) that go to the same stand wait in a queue (linked list). Each stand can hold unlimited buses.
Two dice are rolled: first die gives initial parking spot, second die gives step size to move. This ensures cars with same first roll take different step sizes, reducing crowding.
A busy corridor where shops (keys) cluster together because each new shop extends the existing block of shops - it's easier to build next to existing shops than start a new cluster.

analogies.analogy: Finding a seat in a packed movie theatre - you start at your assigned seat and keep moving to the next available seat until you find one.
Searching for a book in a library where you check the aisle, then skip more shelves each time (1st aisle, 4th aisle, 9th aisle...).
Each apartment building (hash index) has multiple apartments (linked list) - multiple families can live in the same building.
A 'Reserved' sign left on a table in a restaurant after someone leaves, so other diners know someone was here but the seat is now available.
